techniques.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
techniques.evidence_quote: 53 secrets swiped from AWS Secrets Manager

attack_chain.description: Fulcrumsec exploited CVE-2025-55182 (React2Shell), an unpatched vulnerability in a React container running on a LexisNexis AWS instance, to gain initial code execution on the server.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Fulcrumsec reckons it exfiltrated the files from a LexisNexis AWS instance by exploiting a vulnerable React container - specifically, an unpatched React2Shell vulnerability

attack_chain.description: Attackers leveraged the React2Shell container escape vulnerability to break out of the container context and gain access to the broader AWS environment and underlying host resources.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: exploiting a vulnerable React container - specifically, an unpatched React2Shell vulnerability

attack_chain.description: Attackers accessed AWS Secrets Manager and stole 53 secrets, likely obtaining database credentials and other service credentials to enable lateral movement to data stores.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: 53 secrets swiped from AWS Secrets Manager

attack_chain.description: Using harvested credentials, attackers enumerated the AWS environment, discovering VPC databases, Redshift clusters, and other data stores available to the compromised identity.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: 17 VPC databases and more than 430 VPC database tables, 536 Redshift tables, 3.9 million database records

attack_chain.description: Attackers queried and collected data from VPC databases (17 databases, 430+ tables), Redshift clusters (536 tables), and Secrets Manager, aggregating approximately 3.9 million records and 2 GB of data including ~400K user profiles.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Among the other files are 17 VPC databases and more than 430 VPC database tables, 536 Redshift tables, 3.9 million database records, and 53 secrets swiped from AWS Secrets Manager

attack_chain.description: Fulcrumsec exfiltrated the collected data (2 GB haul) from the LexisNexis AWS environment to attacker-controlled infrastructure, later publishing the listing on a data leak site.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Crooks claim 2 GB haul from AWS instance via React2Shell exploit

initial_access_vector: Exploitation of unpatched React2Shell vulnerability (CVE-2025-55182) in a React container hosted on a LexisNexis AWS instance
persistence_mechanism: Not described in the source text
privilege_escalation: Container escape via React2Shell (CVE-2025-55182), followed by credential harvesting from AWS Secrets Manager to access broader cloud resources
